1	-------------------------------- MODULE Sets --------------------------------
2	EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
3	
4	IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
5	                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
6	                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y
7	
8	IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
9	
10	CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
11	AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
12	         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
13	           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
14	                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
15	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	
17	THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
18	  PROOF OMITTED
19	
20	------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
23	           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
24	           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
25	           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
26	  PROOF OMITTED
27	
28	THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
29	    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
30	           NEW x, x \notin S
31	    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
32	           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
33	  PROOF OMITTED
34	
35	------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
38	                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
39	  PROOF OMITTED
40	
41	THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
42	                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
43	                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
44	  PROOF OMITTED
45	
46	THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
47	  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
48	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
49	         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
50	  PROOF OMITTED
51	
52	------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
55	   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
56	   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
57	  PROOF OMITTED
58	
59	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
62	  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
63	         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
64	  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
65	  PROOF OMITTED
66	
67	THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
68	  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
69	           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
70	           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
71	  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
72	  PROOF OMITTED
73	
74	THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
75	  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
76	               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
77	               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))
78	  PROOF OMITTED
79	
80	LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
81	      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
82	             NEW x \in S
83	      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
84	            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
85	  PROOF OMITTED
86	
87	THEOREM FiniteSubset ==
88	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW TT, IsFiniteSet(TT), S \subseteq TT
89	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
90	         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq Cardinality(TT)
91	  PROOF OMITTED
92	
93	-------------------------------------------------------
94	
95	THEOREM CardinalityUnion ==
96	          \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) =>
97	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
98	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
99	                      /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
100	                              Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T)
101	                              - Cardinality(S \cap T)  
102	  PROOF OMITTED
103	
104	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	THEOREM PigeonHole ==
107	            \A S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
108	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
109	                      /\ Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S)
110	                      => \A f \in [S -> T] :
111	                           \E x, y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
112	  PROOF OMITTED
113	
114	-------------------------------------------------------
115	
116	THEOREM \A S, T , f :  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
117	                       /\ f \in [S -> T]
118	                       /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : y = f[x]
119	                       => /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
120	                          /\ Cardinality(T) \leq Cardinality(S)
121	PROOF OMITTED
122	
123	THEOREM ProductFinite ==
124	     \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) => IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
125	PROOF OMITTED
126	
127	THEOREM SubsetsFinite == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
128	PROOF OMITTED
129	=============================================================================

-------------------------------- MODULE Sets --------------------------------
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y

IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))
  PROOF OMITTED

LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
             NEW x \in S
      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM FiniteSubset ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW TT, IsFiniteSet(TT), S \subseteq TT
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq Cardinality(TT)
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityUnion ==
          \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) =>
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
                      /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
                              Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T)
                              - Cardinality(S \cap T)  
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM PigeonHole ==
            \A S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
                      /\ Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S)
                      => \A f \in [S -> T] :
                           \E x, y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM \A S, T , f :  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
                       /\ f \in [S -> T]
                       /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : y = f[x]
                       => /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
                          /\ Cardinality(T) \leq Cardinality(S)
PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM ProductFinite ==
     \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) => IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM SubsetsFinite == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
PROOF OMITTED
=============================================================================

174:(* Theorems about Append                                                   *)
177:THEOREM AppendProperties ==
179:  PROVE  /\ Append(seq, elt) \in Seq(S)
180:         /\ Append(seq, elt) # << >>
181:         /\ Len(Append(seq, elt)) = Len(seq)+1
182:         /\ \A i \in 1.. Len(seq) : Append(seq, elt)[i] = seq[i]
183:         /\ Append(seq, elt)[Len(seq)+1] = elt
184:         /\ Range(Append(seq, elt)) = Range(seq) \cup {elt}
186:THEOREM AppendIsConcat ==
188:  PROVE  Append(seq, elt) = seq \o <<elt>>
190:THEOREM HeadTailAppend ==
192:  PROVE  /\ Head(Append(seq, elt)) = IF seq = <<>> THEN elt ELSE Head(seq)
193:         /\ Tail(Append(seq, elt)) = IF seq = <<>> THEN <<>> ELSE Append(Tail(seq), elt)
195:THEOREM AppendInjective ==
197:  PROVE  Append(s,e) = Append(t,f) <=> s = t /\ e = f
199:THEOREM SequenceEmptyOrAppend ==
201:  PROVE \E s \in Seq(S), elt \in S : seq = Append(s, elt)
207:THEOREM SequencesInductionAppend ==
210:         \A s \in Seq(S), e \in S : P(s) => P(Append(s,e))
254:LEMMA AppendInjectiveSeq ==
256:  PROVE  IsInjective(Append(seq,e)) <=> IsInjective(seq) /\ e \notin Range(seq)

1	----------------------- MODULE SequenceTheorems_proofs ----------------------
2	(***************************************************************************)
3	(* This module contains the proofs for theorems about sequences and the    *)
4	(* corresponding operations.                                               *)
5	(***************************************************************************)
6	EXTENDS Sequences, Functions
7	LOCAL INSTANCE NaturalsInduction
8	LOCAL INSTANCE TLAPS
9	
10	
11	(***************************************************************************)
12	(* Elementary properties about Seq(S)                                      *)
13	(***************************************************************************)
14	
15	LEMMA SeqDef == \A S : Seq(S) = UNION {[1..n -> S] : n \in Nat}
16	OBVIOUS
17	
18	THEOREM SeqMonotonic ==
19	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
20	         S \subseteq T
21	  PROVE  seq \in Seq(T)
22	OBVIOUS
23	
24	THEOREM ElementOfSeq ==
25	   ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
26	          NEW n \in 1..Len(seq)
27	   PROVE  seq[n] \in S
28	OBVIOUS
29	
30	THEOREM EmptySeq ==
31	   ASSUME NEW S
32	   PROVE /\ << >> \in Seq(S)
33	         /\ Len(<< >>) = 0
34	         /\ \A seq \in Seq(S) : (seq # << >>) => (Len(seq) \in Nat \ {0})
35	OBVIOUS
36	
37	THEOREM LenProperties ==
38	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
39	  PROVE  /\ Len(seq) \in Nat
40	         /\ seq \in [1..Len(seq) -> S]
41	         /\ DOMAIN seq = 1 .. Len(seq)
42	OBVIOUS
43	
44	THEOREM IsASeq ==
45	  ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW e(_), NEW S,
46	         \A i \in 1..n : e(i) \in S
47	  PROVE  [i \in 1..n |-> e(i)] \in Seq(S)
48	OBVIOUS
49	
50	THEOREM ExceptSeq ==
51	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW e \in S
52	  PROVE  /\ [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e] \in Seq(S)
53	         /\ Len([seq EXCEPT ![i] = e]) = Len(seq)
54	         /\ \A j \in 1 .. Len(seq) : [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e][j] = IF j=i THEN e ELSE seq[j]
55	OBVIOUS
56	
57	THEOREM SeqEqual ==
58	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S),
59	         Len(s) = Len(t), \A i \in 1 .. Len(s) : s[i] = t[i]
60	  PROVE  s = t
61	OBVIOUS
62	
63	(**************************************************************************)
64	(* Properties of concatenation (\o)                                       *)
65	(**************************************************************************)
66	
67	THEOREM ConcatProperties ==
68	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s1 \in Seq(S), NEW s2 \in Seq(S)
69	  PROVE  /\ s1 \o s2 \in Seq(S)
70	         /\ Len(s1 \o s2) = Len(s1) + Len(s2)
71	         /\ \A i \in 1 .. Len(s1) + Len(s2) : (s1 \o s2)[i] =
72	                     IF i <= Len(s1) THEN s1[i] ELSE s2[i - Len(s1)]
73	OBVIOUS
74	
75	THEOREM ConcatEmptySeq ==
76	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
77	  PROVE  /\ seq \o << >> = seq
78	         /\ << >> \o seq = seq
79	OBVIOUS
80	
81	THEOREM ConcatEqualIffEmpty ==
82	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S)
83	  PROVE  /\ s \o t = s <=> t = << >>
84	         /\ s \o t = t <=> s = << >>
85	         /\ s \o t = << >> <=> s = << >> /\ t = << >>
86	OBVIOUS
87	
88	THEOREM ConcatAssociative ==
89	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S), NEW u \in Seq(S)
90	  PROVE  (s \o t) \o u = s \o (t \o u)
91	OBVIOUS
92	
93	(****************************************************************************)
94	(* Intentionally not written as an ASSUME ... PROVE such that the conjuncts *)
95	(* can be named individually.                                               *)
96	(****************************************************************************)
97	THEOREM ConcatSimplifications ==
98	  /\ idRight:: \A S : \A s,t \in Seq(S) : s \o t = s => t = <<>>
99	  /\ idLeft:: \A S : \A s,t \in Seq(S) : s \o t = t => s = <<>>
100	  /\ concIsEmpty:: \A S : \A s,t \in Seq(S) : s \o t = <<>> => s = <<>> /\ t = <<>>
101	  /\ cancelLeft:: \A S : \A s,t,v \in Seq(S) : s \o t = s \o v => t = v
102	  /\ cancelRight:: \A S : \A s,t,v \in Seq(S) : s \o v = t \o v => s = t
103	<1>1. ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S), NEW v \in Seq(S),
104	             s \o t = s \o v
105	      PROVE  t = v
106	  <2>1. Len(t) = Len(v)
107	    BY <1>1
108	  <2>2. ASSUME NEW i \in 1 .. Len(t) PROVE t[i] = v[i]
109	    <3>1. t[i] = (s \o t)[Len(s)+i]
110	      OBVIOUS
111	    <3>2. v[i] = (s \o v)[Len(s)+i]
112	      BY <2>1
113	    <3>. QED  BY <1>1, <3>1, <3>2
114	  <2>. QED  BY <2>1, <2>2
115	<1>2. ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S), NEW v \in Seq(S),
116	             s \o v = t \o v
117	      PROVE  s = t
118	  <2>1. Len(s) = Len(t)
119	    BY <1>2
120	  <2>2. ASSUME NEW i \in 1 .. Len(s) PROVE s[i] = t[i]
121	    BY <1>2, <2>1
122	  <2>. QED  BY <2>1, <2>2
123	<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, ConcatEqualIffEmpty
124	
125	(***************************************************************************)
126	(*                     SubSeq, Head and Tail                               *)
127	(***************************************************************************)
128	
129	THEOREM SubSeqProperties ==
130	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in Int, NEW n \in Int,
131	         m > n \/ (1 <= m /\ n <= Len(s)),
132	         \A i \in m .. n : s[i] \in S
133	   PROVE /\ SubSeq(s,m,n) \in Seq(S)
134	         /\ Len(SubSeq(s,m,n)) = IF m<=n THEN n-m+1 ELSE 0
135	         /\ \A i \in 1 .. n-m+1 : SubSeq(s,m,n)[i] = s[m+i-1]
136	OBVIOUS
137	
138	THEOREM SubSeqEmpty ==
139	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in Int, NEW n \in Int, n < m
140	  PROVE  SubSeq(s,m,n) = << >>
141	OBVIOUS
142	
143	THEOREM SubSeqRange ==
144	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in 1 .. Len(s), NEW n \in 1 .. Len(s)
145	  PROVE  Range(SubSeq(s,m,n)) \subseteq Range(s)
146	BY DEF Range
147	
148	THEOREM HeadTailProperties ==
149	   ASSUME NEW S,
150	          NEW s \in Seq(S), s # << >>
151	   PROVE  /\ Head(s) \in S
152	          /\ Head(s) \in Range(s)
153	          /\ Tail(s) \in Seq(S)
154	          /\ Len(Tail(s)) = Len(s)-1
155	          /\ \A i \in 1 .. Len(Tail(s)) : Tail(s)[i] = s[i+1]
156	          /\ Range(Tail(s)) \subseteq Range(s)
157	BY DEF Range
158	
159	THEOREM TailIsSubSeq ==
160	  ASSUME NEW S,
161	         NEW s \in Seq(S), s # << >>
162	  PROVE  Tail(s) = SubSeq(s, 2, Len(s))
163	OBVIOUS
164	
165	THEOREM SubSeqRestrict ==
166	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW n \in 0 .. Len(seq)
167	  PROVE  SubSeq(seq, 1, n) = Restrict(seq, 1 .. n)
168	BY DEF Restrict
169	
170	THEOREM HeadTailOfSubSeq ==
171	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
172	         NEW m \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW n \in m .. Len(seq)
173	  PROVE  /\ Head(SubSeq(seq,m,n)) = seq[m]
174	         /\ Tail(SubSeq(seq,m,n)) = SubSeq(seq, m+1, n)
175	OBVIOUS
176	
177	THEOREM SubSeqRecursive ==
178	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
179	         NEW m \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW n \in m .. Len(seq)
180	  PROVE  /\ SubSeq(seq, m, n) = << seq[m] >> \o SubSeq(seq, m+1, n)
181	         /\ SubSeq(seq, m, n) = SubSeq(seq, m, n-1) \o << seq[n] >>
182	OBVIOUS
183	
184	(***************************************************************************)
185	(* Subsequences of subsequences.                                           *)
186	(***************************************************************************)
187	THEOREM SubSeqOfSubSeq ==
188	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S),
189	         NEW i \in 1 .. Len(s), NEW j \in 1 .. Len(s),
190	         NEW m \in 1 .. j-i+1, NEW n \in 1 .. j-i+1
191	  PROVE  SubSeq(SubSeq(s,i,j),m,n) = SubSeq(s, i+m-1, i+n-1)
192	OBVIOUS
193	
194	(*****************************************************************************)
195	(* Adjacent subsequences can be concatenated to obtain a longer subsequence. *)
196	(*****************************************************************************)
197	THEOREM ConcatAdjacentSubSeq ==
198	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
199	         NEW m \in 1 .. Len(seq)+1,

----------------------- MODULE SequenceTheorems_proofs ----------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* This module contains the proofs for theorems about sequences and the    *)
(* corresponding operations.                                               *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Sequences, Functions
LOCAL INSTANCE NaturalsInduction
LOCAL INSTANCE TLAPS


(***************************************************************************)
(* Elementary properties about Seq(S)                                      *)
(***************************************************************************)

LEMMA SeqDef == \A S : Seq(S) = UNION {[1..n -> S] : n \in Nat}
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SeqMonotonic ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
         S \subseteq T
  PROVE  seq \in Seq(T)
OBVIOUS

THEOREM ElementOfSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
          NEW n \in 1..Len(seq)
   PROVE  seq[n] \in S
OBVIOUS

THEOREM EmptySeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S
   PROVE /\ << >> \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(<< >>) = 0
         /\ \A seq \in Seq(S) : (seq # << >>) => (Len(seq) \in Nat \ {0})
OBVIOUS

THEOREM LenProperties ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ Len(seq) \in Nat
         /\ seq \in [1..Len(seq) -> S]
         /\ DOMAIN seq = 1 .. Len(seq)
OBVIOUS

THEOREM IsASeq ==
  ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW e(_), NEW S,
         \A i \in 1..n : e(i) \in S
  PROVE  [i \in 1..n |-> e(i)] \in Seq(S)
OBVIOUS

THEOREM ExceptSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW e \in S
  PROVE  /\ [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e] \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len([seq EXCEPT ![i] = e]) = Len(seq)
         /\ \A j \in 1 .. Len(seq) : [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e][j] = IF j=i THEN e ELSE seq[j]
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SeqEqual ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S),
         Len(s) = Len(t), \A i \in 1 .. Len(s) : s[i] = t[i]
  PROVE  s = t
OBVIOUS

(**************************************************************************)
(* Properties of concatenation (\o)                                       *)
(**************************************************************************)

THEOREM ConcatProperties ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s1 \in Seq(S), NEW s2 \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ s1 \o s2 \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(s1 \o s2) = Len(s1) + Len(s2)
         /\ \A i \in 1 .. Len(s1) + Len(s2) : (s1 \o s2)[i] =
                     IF i <= Len(s1) THEN s1[i] ELSE s2[i - Len(s1)]
OBVIOUS

THEOREM ConcatEmptySeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ seq \o << >> = seq
         /\ << >> \o seq = seq
OBVIOUS

THEOREM ConcatEqualIffEmpty ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ s \o t = s <=> t = << >>
         /\ s \o t = t <=> s = << >>
         /\ s \o t = << >> <=> s = << >> /\ t = << >>
OBVIOUS

THEOREM ConcatAssociative ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S), NEW u \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  (s \o t) \o u = s \o (t \o u)
OBVIOUS

  /\ idLeft:: \A S : \A s,t \in Seq(S) : s \o t = t => s = <<>>
  /\ concIsEmpty:: \A S : \A s,t \in Seq(S) : s \o t = <<>> => s = <<>> /\ t = <<>>
  /\ cancelLeft:: \A S : \A s,t,v \in Seq(S) : s \o t = s \o v => t = v
  /\ cancelRight:: \A S : \A s,t,v \in Seq(S) : s \o v = t \o v => s = t
<1>1. ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S), NEW v \in Seq(S),
             s \o t = s \o v
      PROVE  t = v

THEOREM SubSeqProperties ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in Int, NEW n \in Int,
         m > n \/ (1 <= m /\ n <= Len(s)),
         \A i \in m .. n : s[i] \in S
   PROVE /\ SubSeq(s,m,n) \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(SubSeq(s,m,n)) = IF m<=n THEN n-m+1 ELSE 0
         /\ \A i \in 1 .. n-m+1 : SubSeq(s,m,n)[i] = s[m+i-1]
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SubSeqEmpty ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in Int, NEW n \in Int, n < m
  PROVE  SubSeq(s,m,n) = << >>
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SubSeqRange ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW m \in 1 .. Len(s), NEW n \in 1 .. Len(s)
  PROVE  Range(SubSeq(s,m,n)) \subseteq Range(s)
BY DEF Range

THEOREM HeadTailProperties ==
   ASSUME NEW S,
          NEW s \in Seq(S), s # << >>
   PROVE  /\ Head(s) \in S
          /\ Head(s) \in Range(s)
          /\ Tail(s) \in Seq(S)
          /\ Len(Tail(s)) = Len(s)-1
          /\ \A i \in 1 .. Len(Tail(s)) : Tail(s)[i] = s[i+1]
          /\ Range(Tail(s)) \subseteq Range(s)
BY DEF Range

THEOREM TailIsSubSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S,
         NEW s \in Seq(S), s # << >>
  PROVE  Tail(s) = SubSeq(s, 2, Len(s))
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SubSeqRestrict ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW n \in 0 .. Len(seq)
  PROVE  SubSeq(seq, 1, n) = Restrict(seq, 1 .. n)
BY DEF Restrict

THEOREM HeadTailOfSubSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
         NEW m \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW n \in m .. Len(seq)
  PROVE  /\ Head(SubSeq(seq,m,n)) = seq[m]
         /\ Tail(SubSeq(seq,m,n)) = SubSeq(seq, m+1, n)
OBVIOUS

THEOREM SubSeqRecursive ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
         NEW m \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW n \in m .. Len(seq)
  PROVE  /\ SubSeq(seq, m, n) = << seq[m] >> \o SubSeq(seq, m+1, n)
         /\ SubSeq(seq, m, n) = SubSeq(seq, m, n-1) \o << seq[n] >>
OBVIOUS

(* Subsequences of subsequences.                                           *)

THEOREM SubSeqOfSubSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S),
         NEW i \in 1 .. Len(s), NEW j \in 1 .. Len(s),
         NEW m \in 1 .. j-i+1, NEW n \in 1 .. j-i+1
  PROVE  SubSeq(SubSeq(s,i,j),m,n) = SubSeq(s, i+m-1, i+n-1)
OBVIOUS

  <2>. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW s \in Seq(S), Len(s) = n+1
                PROVE  P(s)
    OBVIOUS
  <2>1. /\ Tail(s) \in Seq(S)
        /\ Len(Tail(s)) = n
    OBVIOUS
  <2>. QED  BY <2>1, <1>2, Zenon
<1>3. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, NatInduction, Isa


(***************************************************************************)
(*                          RANGE OF SEQUENCE                              *)
(***************************************************************************)

THEOREM RangeOfSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  Range(seq) \in SUBSET S
BY DEF Range

THEOREM RangeEquality ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE Range(seq) = { seq[i] : i \in 1 .. Len(seq) }
BY DEF Range

THEOREM SeqOfRange ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  seq \in Seq(Range(seq))
BY DEF Range

(* The range of the concatenation of two sequences is the union of the ranges *)
THEOREM RangeConcatenation ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s1 \in Seq(S), NEW s2 \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  Range(s1 \o s2) = Range(s1) \cup Range(s2)
<1>1. Range(s1) \subseteq Range(s1 \o s2)
  BY DEF Range
<1>2. Range(s2) \subseteq Range(s1 \o s2)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW i \in 1 .. Len(s2)
                 PROVE  s2[i] \in Range(s1 \o s2)
    BY RangeEquality
  <2>2. /\ Len(s1)+i \in 1 .. Len(s1 \o s2)
        /\ (s1 \o s2)[Len(s1)+i] = s2[i]
    OBVIOUS
  <2>. QED  BY <2>2 DEF Range
<1>3. Range(s1 \o s2) \subseteq Range(s1) \cup Range(s2)
  BY DEF Range
<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3

(***************************************************************************)
(* Theorems about injective sequences, i.e. sequences without duplicates.  *)
(***************************************************************************)

LEMMA EmptySingletonIsInjective ==
  /\ IsInjective(<< >>)
  /\ \A e : IsInjective(<< e >>)
BY DEF IsInjective

LEMMA ConcatInjectiveSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  IsInjective(s \o t) <=>
         IsInjective(s) /\ IsInjective(t) /\ Range(s) \cap Range(t) = {}
<1>. DEFINE st == s \o t
<1>1. ASSUME IsInjective(st)  PROVE IsInjective(s)
  BY <1>1 DEF IsInjective
<1>2. ASSUME IsInjective(st)  PROVE IsInjective(t)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW i \in DOMAIN t, NEW j \in DOMAIN t, t[i] = t[j]
                 PROVE  i = j
    BY DEF IsInjective
  <2>2. /\ i + Len(s) \in DOMAIN st
        /\ j + Len(s) \in DOMAIN st
        /\ st[i+Len(s)] = st[j + Len(s)]
    BY <2>1
  <2>. QED  BY <1>2, <2>2 DEF IsInjective
<1>3. ASSUME IsInjective(st), NEW e \in Range(s) \cap Range(t)
      PROVE  FALSE
  <2>1. PICK i \in DOMAIN s : s[i] = e
    BY <1>3 DEF Range
  <2>2. PICK j \in DOMAIN t : t[j] = e
    BY <1>3 DEF Range
  <2>3. /\ i \in DOMAIN st /\ st[i] = e
        /\ j+Len(s) \in DOMAIN st /\ st[j+Len(s)] = e
    BY <2>1, <2>2
  <2>. QED  BY <1>3, <2>3 DEF IsInjective
<1>4. ASSUME IsInjective(s), IsInjective(t), Range(s) \cap Range(t) = {}
      PROVE  IsInjective(st)
  BY <1>4 DEF IsInjective, Range
<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4

LEMMA AppendInjectiveSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW e \in S
  PROVE  IsInjective(Append(seq,e)) <=> IsInjective(seq) /\ e \notin Range(seq)
<1>. DEFINE app == Append(seq,e)
<1>1. ASSUME IsInjective(app)
      PROVE  IsInjective(seq)
  BY <1>1 DEF IsInjective
<1>2. ASSUME IsInjective(app), e \in Range(seq)
      PROVE  FALSE
  <2>1. PICK i \in DOMAIN seq : seq[i] = e
    BY <1>2 DEF Range
  <2>2. app[i] = e /\ app[Len(seq)+1] = e
    BY <2>1
  <2>. QED
    BY <1>2, <2>2 DEF IsInjective
<1>3. ASSUME IsInjective(seq), e \notin Range(seq)
      PROVE  IsInjective(app)
  BY <1>3 DEF IsInjective, Range
<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3

LEMMA TailInjectiveSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), IsInjective(seq), seq # << >>
  PROVE  /\ IsInjective(Tail(seq))
         /\ Range(Tail(seq)) = Range(seq) \ {Head(seq)}
<1>. DEFINE tl == Tail(seq)
<1>1. IsInjective(tl)
  BY DEF IsInjective
<1>2. ASSUME NEW e \in Range(tl)
      PROVE  e \in Range(seq) \ {Head(seq)}
  BY DEF Range, IsInjective
<1>3. ASSUME NEW e \in Range(seq) \ {Head(seq)}
      PROVE  e \in Range(tl)
  <2>1. PICK i \in 2 .. Len(seq) : seq[i] = e
    BY DEF Range
  <2>2. i-1 \in DOMAIN tl /\ tl[i-1] = e
    BY <2>1
  <2>. QED  BY <2>2, Zenon DEF Range
<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3

-------------------------- MODULE SequenceTheorems --------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* This module contains theorems about operations on sequences.            *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Sequences, Functions
LOCAL INSTANCE NaturalsInduction
LOCAL INSTANCE TLAPS


(***************************************************************************)
(* Elementary properties about Seq(S)                                      *)
(***************************************************************************)

LEMMA SeqDef == \A S : Seq(S) = UNION {[1..n -> S] : n \in Nat}

THEOREM SeqMonotonic ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
         S \subseteq T
  PROVE  seq \in Seq(T)

THEOREM ElementOfSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S),
          NEW n \in 1..Len(seq)
   PROVE  seq[n] \in S

THEOREM EmptySeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S
   PROVE /\ << >> \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(<< >>) = 0
         /\ \A seq \in Seq(S) : (seq # << >>) => (Len(seq) \in Nat \ {0})

THEOREM LenProperties ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ Len(seq) \in Nat
         /\ seq \in [1..Len(seq) -> S]
         /\ DOMAIN seq = 1 .. Len(seq)

THEOREM IsASeq ==
  ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW e(_), NEW S,
         \A i \in 1..n : e(i) \in S
  PROVE  [i \in 1..n |-> e(i)] \in Seq(S)

THEOREM ExceptSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1 .. Len(seq), NEW e \in S
  PROVE  /\ [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e] \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len([seq EXCEPT ![i] = e]) = Len(seq)
         /\ \A j \in 1 .. Len(seq) : [seq EXCEPT ![i] = e][j] = IF j=i THEN e ELSE seq[j]

THEOREM SeqEqual ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW t \in Seq(S),
         Len(s) = Len(t), \A i \in 1 .. Len(s) : s[i] = t[i]
  PROVE  s = t

(**************************************************************************)
(* Properties of concatenation (\o)                                       *)
(**************************************************************************)

THEOREM ConcatProperties ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s1 \in Seq(S), NEW s2 \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  /\ s1 \o s2 \in Seq(S)
